# System Architecture Documentation ## ChronicCareAIPlatform - Chronic Disease Care Management Platform **Document Version**: 1.0 **Generation Date**: 2025-01-09 **Architecture Style**: Microservices with Dubbo RPC **Technology Foundation**: Spring Boot 3.x, Java 17 --- ## 1. Architecture Overview ### 1.1 Architecture Design Philosophy The ChronicCareAIPlatform is architected following a **domain-driven microservices** approach, emphasizing clear separation of concerns, interface-driven development, and robust security at every layer. The platform addresses the complex requirements of chronic disease management by providing a scalable, maintainable, and secure foundation for healthcare digital transformation. **Core Design Principles:** | Principle | Implementation | |-----------|----------------| | **Interface-Driven Development** | API modules define contracts that both consumers and providers implement, ensuring type-safe RPC communication | | **Separation of Concerns** | Three-tier module pattern (API/Consumer/Provider) cleanly separates HTTP handling from business logic | | **Security by Design** | Multi-layered security with JWT authentication, RBAC authorization, and AOP-based permission enforcement | | **Loose Coupling** | Services communicate through well-defined Dubbo interfaces, enabling independent deployment and scaling | | **Domain Isolation** | Each business domain encapsulates its own data, logic, and API contracts | ### 1.2 Core Architecture Patterns The platform implements several proven architectural patterns that work together to create a cohesive, maintainable system: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Architectural Patterns" P1[Three-Tier Module Pattern
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
API → Consumer → Provider
Interface contracts shared
across service boundaries] P2[Gateway Pattern
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Centralized entry point
Cross-cutting concerns
Security enforcement] P3[BFF Pattern
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Consumer modules as
Backend-for-Frontend
REST to RPC translation] P4[Dual-Token Auth
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Access Token: 30 min
Refresh Token: 7 days
Redis-backed sessions] end subgraph "Supporting Patterns" P5[Repository Pattern
MyBatis-Plus DAL] P6[Adapter Pattern
Third-party integrations] P7[Interceptor Chain
User context propagation] P8[AOP Security
Declarative permissions] end P1 --> P5 P2 --> P7 P3 --> P1 P4 --> P8 ``` **Three-Tier Module Pattern Deep Dive:** Each business domain follows a consistent three-tier structure that promotes maintainability and clear boundaries: ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "API Module (-api)" A1[Dubbo Service Interfaces] A2[DTOs - Request/Response] A3[Persistent Objects - PO] A4[Value Objects - VO] A5[Enumerations] end subgraph "Consumer Module (-consumer)" C1[REST Controllers] C2[Security Annotations] C3[Request Validation] C4[Response Formatting] C5[BFF Orchestration] end subgraph "Provider Module (-provider)" P1[Dubbo Service Impl] P2[Business Logic] P3[Data Access - Mappers] P4[External Service Calls] P5[Transaction Management] end C1 -->|"Dubbo RPC"| P1 C1 -->|"implements"| A1 P1 -->|"implements"| A1 C2 --> A2 P2 --> A3 P2 --> A4 ``` ### 1.3 Technology Stack Overview The platform leverages a modern, production-ready technology stack optimized for healthcare applications: | Category | Technology | Version | Purpose | |----------|------------|---------|---------| | **Runtime** | Java | 17 | Language runtime with modern features | | **Framework** | Spring Boot | 3.5.9 | Application framework and auto-configuration | | **RPC Framework** | Apache Dubbo | 3.3.0 | High-performance service communication | | **API Gateway** | Spring Cloud Gateway | 2025.0.1 | Request routing and filtering | | **Service Discovery** | Alibaba Nacos | 3.1.1 | Service registry and configuration center | | **Rate Limiting** | Alibaba Sentinel | - | Flow control and circuit breaking | | **Database** | MySQL | - | Primary relational data store | | **ORM** | MyBatis-Plus | - | Enhanced data access layer | | **Cache** | Redis | - | Sessions, tokens, and caching | | **Security** | Spring Security + JWT | - | Authentication framework | | **JWT Library** | Nimbus JOSE+JWT | - | Token generation and validation | | **Cloud Storage** | Alibaba Cloud OSS | - | Health report file storage | --- ## 2. System Context ### 2.1 System Positioning and Value The ChronicCareAIPlatform serves as a comprehensive digital health management solution designed specifically for chronic disease care scenarios. It bridges the gap between patients requiring ongoing medical attention and healthcare providers managing complex care workflows. **Business Value Proposition:** ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Value Delivery" V1[Streamlined Care
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Digital health records
Automated scheduling
AI-assisted planning] V2[Reduced Burden
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Less administrative work
Efficient workflows
Automated notifications] V3[Better Engagement
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Patient self-service
Multi-channel comms
Care continuity] end V1 --> OUTCOME[Improved Patient Outcomes
& Healthcare Efficiency] V2 --> OUTCOME V3 --> OUTCOME ``` ### 2.2 User Roles and Scenarios The platform supports four distinct user roles, each with specific capabilities and access patterns: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Primary Users" PATIENT[👤 Patient
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Upload health reports
• View personal records
• Access doctor info
• Receive notifications] DOCTOR[👨‍⚕️ Doctor
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• View patient reports
• Manage schedules
• AI recommendations
• Complete consultations] end subgraph "Secondary Users" FAMILY[👨‍👩‍👧 Family Member
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• View authorized info
• Emergency notifications
• Limited access rights] ADMIN[🔧 Administrator
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Manage accounts
• Configure permissions
• Monitor system health] end PATIENT -->|"Assigned to"| DOCTOR FAMILY -->|"Authorized by"| PATIENT ADMIN -->|"Manages"| PATIENT ADMIN -->|"Manages"| DOCTOR ``` **Role-Permission Matrix:** | Role | Permission Scope | Key Capabilities | |------|------------------|------------------| | **PATIENT** | PATIENT:READ, PATIENT:WRITE | Own health records, report uploads, profile management | | **DOCTOR** | DOCTOR:READ, DOCTOR:WRITE, DOCTOR:ALL | Patient records (assigned), schedules, diagnostics | | **FAMILY** | FAMILY:READ | Authorized patient information only | | **ADMIN** | ALL:ALL | Full system access for administration | ### 2.3 External System Interactions The platform integrates with multiple external systems to provide comprehensive functionality: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "ChronicCareAIPlatform" GW[API Gateway] SERVICES[Microservices] end subgraph "Cloud Infrastructure" OSS[Alibaba Cloud OSS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Health report storage
Images, PDFs, documents] NACOS[Nacos
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Service discovery
Configuration center] SENTINEL[Sentinel
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Rate limiting
Circuit breaking] end subgraph "Data Infrastructure" MYSQL[(MySQL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Business data
User accounts
Health records)] REDIS[(Redis
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
JWT tokens
Session cache
Blacklists)] end subgraph "Communication Services" SMS[SMS Gateway
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Notifications
Verification codes] EMAIL[Email Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Notifications
Attachments] end GW --> NACOS GW --> SENTINEL SERVICES --> OSS SERVICES --> MYSQL SERVICES --> REDIS SERVICES --> SMS SERVICES --> EMAIL ``` **Integration Protocols:** | External System | Protocol | Purpose | |-----------------|----------|---------| | Alibaba Cloud OSS | REST API | File upload/download for health reports | | SMS Gateway | REST API | Notification delivery with rate limiting | | Email Service | SMTP/REST | Email notifications and attachments | | Nacos | Service Discovery Protocol | Service registration and configuration | | Redis | Redis Protocol | Token storage, caching, blacklists | | MySQL | JDBC via MyBatis-Plus | Persistent data storage | | Sentinel | SDK Integration | Flow control and circuit breaking | ### 2.4 System Boundary Definition The platform has clearly defined boundaries that establish what is included and excluded from the system scope: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "System Boundary - Included" direction TB INC1[API Gateway
Routing, Auth, Rate Limiting] INC2[Account Management
Users, Roles, Permissions] INC3[Auth Server
JWT, SSO, Token Management] INC4[Health Report Center
Upload, Storage, Audit] INC5[Doctor Service
Schedules, AI, Workbench] INC6[Message System
Push, SMS, Email] INC7[Third-Party Service
OSS, SMS, Email Adapters] INC8[Utils Module
Security, Encryption, Context] end subgraph "Outside Boundary - Excluded" direction TB EXC1[AI/ML Training Infrastructure] EXC2[Hospital Information Systems] EXC3[Medical Device Integration] EXC4[Payment & Billing] EXC5[Video Consultation] EXC6[Mobile/Web Clients] EXC7[Analytics Dashboards] end INC1 -.->|"Future Integration"| EXC2 INC5 -.->|"Future Enhancement"| EXC1 ``` --- ## 3. Container View ### 3.1 Domain Module Division The platform is organized into eight distinct domain modules, each with clear responsibilities and boundaries: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Infrastructure Domains" GW_DOMAIN[API Gateway Domain
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.0/10
Complexity: 7.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• JWT Validation
• Rate Limiting
• User Context Injection
• CORS Configuration] UTILS_DOMAIN[Common Utilities Domain
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 8.5/10
Complexity: 6.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• UserInfoManager
• Security Annotations
• Data Encryption
• XSS/SQL Filters] THIRD_DOMAIN[Third-Party Integration
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 7.0/10
Complexity: 5.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• OSS Service
• SMS Service
• Email Service] end subgraph "Core Business Domains" AUTH_DOMAIN[Authentication Domain
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.5/10
Complexity: 8.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• JWT Token Management
• Token Validation
• SSO Service] ACC_DOMAIN[Account Management
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.0/10
Complexity: 7.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• User Registration
• Login Verification
• Permission Service
• User Identity] HEALTH_DOMAIN[Health Report Center
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.0/10
Complexity: 7.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Report Upload
• Report Query
• Audit Workflow] DOC_DOMAIN[Doctor Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 8.5/10
Complexity: 7.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Schedule Management
• AI Recommendations
• Workbench APIs] end subgraph "Supporting Domains" MSG_DOMAIN[Message System
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 7.5/10
Complexity: 6.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Push Notifications
• SMS Delivery
• Email Delivery] end ``` ### 3.2 Domain Module Architecture The complete container architecture shows how all modules interact: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "External Clients" WEB[Web Application] MOBILE[Mobile App] end subgraph "Infrastructure Layer" GW[API Gateway
Spring Cloud Gateway] NACOS[(Nacos
Service Registry)] SENTINEL[Sentinel
Rate Limiting] REDIS[(Redis
Cache/Sessions)] end subgraph "Authentication & Authorization" AUTH_CONSUMER[Auth Consumer
SSO Controller] AUTH_PROVIDER[Auth Provider
JWT Service] end subgraph "Core Business Services" ACC_PROVIDER[Account Provider
User & RBAC] HEALTH_CONSUMER[Health Report Consumer] HEALTH_PROVIDER[Health Report Provider] DOC_CONSUMER[Doctor Service Consumer] DOC_PROVIDER[Doctor Service Provider] end subgraph "Supporting Services" MSG_CONSUMER[Message Consumer] MSG_PROVIDER[Message Provider] THIRD_PROVIDER[Third-Party Provider
OSS/SMS/Email] end subgraph "External Systems" OSS[Alibaba Cloud OSS] SMS_GW[SMS Gateway] EMAIL_SVC[Email Service] MYSQL[(MySQL Database)] end WEB --> GW MOBILE --> GW GW --> NACOS GW --> SENTINEL GW --> AUTH_PROVIDER GW --> AUTH_CONSUMER GW --> HEALTH_CONSUMER GW --> DOC_CONSUMER GW --> MSG_CONSUMER AUTH_CONSUMER --> AUTH_PROVIDER AUTH_CONSUMER --> ACC_PROVIDER AUTH_PROVIDER --> ACC_PROVIDER AUTH_PROVIDER --> REDIS HEALTH_CONSUMER --> HEALTH_PROVIDER HEALTH_PROVIDER --> ACC_PROVIDER HEALTH_PROVIDER --> THIRD_PROVIDER DOC_CONSUMER --> DOC_PROVIDER MSG_CONSUMER --> MSG_PROVIDER MSG_PROVIDER --> ACC_PROVIDER MSG_PROVIDER --> THIRD_PROVIDER THIRD_PROVIDER --> OSS THIRD_PROVIDER --> SMS_GW THIRD_PROVIDER --> EMAIL_SVC ACC_PROVIDER --> MYSQL HEALTH_PROVIDER --> MYSQL DOC_PROVIDER --> MYSQL MSG_PROVIDER --> MYSQL ``` ### 3.3 Storage Design The platform employs a multi-tier storage strategy optimized for different data access patterns: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Primary Storage - MySQL" DB_ACCOUNT[(Account Schema
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• account
• doctor
• patient
• role
• permission
• account_role
• role_permission)] DB_HEALTH[(Health Schema
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• health_report
• report_category
• audit_log)] DB_DOCTOR[(Doctor Schema
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• doctor_schedule
• schedule_category)] DB_MESSAGE[(Message Schema
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• message
• message_recipient
• message_template)] end subgraph "Cache Layer - Redis" REDIS_AUTH[Auth Cache
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}
• blacklist:{tokenId}
• permissions:{userId}] REDIS_SESSION[Session Cache
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• user_session:{userId}
• rate_limit:{key}] end subgraph "Object Storage - OSS" OSS_REPORTS[Health Reports
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Images (PNG, JPG)
• Documents (PDF)
• Organized by date/user] end ``` **Database Schema Overview:** ```mermaid erDiagram ACCOUNT ||--o{ ACCOUNT_ROLE : has ACCOUNT_ROLE }o--|| ROLE : references ROLE ||--o{ ROLE_PERMISSION : has ROLE_PERMISSION }o--|| PERMISSION : references ACCOUNT ||--o| DOCTOR : extends ACCOUNT ||--o| PATIENT : extends PATIENT ||--o{ HEALTH_REPORT : uploads DOCTOR ||--o{ HEALTH_REPORT : reviews DOCTOR ||--o{ DOCTOR_SCHEDULE : manages ACCOUNT ||--o{ MESSAGE : sends ACCOUNT ||--o{ MESSAGE_RECIPIENT : receives MESSAGE ||--o{ MESSAGE_RECIPIENT : has ACCOUNT { bigint account_id PK varchar username UK varchar password varchar phone "AES encrypted" varchar phone_hash UK "SHA-256 for lookup" varchar id_card "AES encrypted" varchar id_card_hash UK varchar email varchar nickname int gender date birthday varchar avatar_url varchar address json ext } DOCTOR { bigint doctor_id PK bigint account_id FK varchar license_number varchar specialty bigint department_id varchar title } PATIENT { bigint patient_id PK bigint account_id FK bigint attending_doctor_id FK varchar medical_record_no text medical_history } HEALTH_REPORT { bigint report_id PK bigint patient_id FK bigint attending_doctor_id FK int report_type varchar category varchar title text description varchar file_url text text_content date report_date int status bigint uploader_id } DOCTOR_SCHEDULE { bigint schedule_id PK bigint doctor_id FK bigint patient_id FK date schedule_day time start_time time end_time int schedule_category int priority int status text diagnostic_report text prescription text notes int version "Optimistic lock" } ``` ### 3.4 Inter-Domain Module Communication Domain modules communicate through well-defined Dubbo RPC interfaces: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "API Contracts (-api modules)" ACC_API[account-api] AUTH_API[auth-api] HEALTH_API[health-center-api] DOC_API[doctor-service-api] MSG_API[message-system-api] THIRD_API[third-party-api] end subgraph "Consumer Layer (BFF)" AUTH_CON[auth-consumer] HEALTH_CON[health-center-consumer] DOC_CON[doctor-service-consumer] MSG_CON[message-system-consumer] end subgraph "Provider Layer (Business Logic)" ACC_PROV[account-provider] AUTH_PROV[auth-provider] HEALTH_PROV[health-center-provider] DOC_PROV[doctor-service-provider] MSG_PROV[message-system-provider] THIRD_PROV[third-party-provider] end subgraph "Shared Infrastructure" UTILS[utils] GATEWAY[gateway] end AUTH_CON --> AUTH_API AUTH_CON --> ACC_API AUTH_PROV --> AUTH_API HEALTH_CON --> HEALTH_API HEALTH_PROV --> HEALTH_API HEALTH_PROV --> ACC_API HEALTH_PROV --> THIRD_API DOC_CON --> DOC_API DOC_PROV --> DOC_API MSG_CON --> MSG_API MSG_PROV --> MSG_API MSG_PROV --> ACC_API MSG_PROV --> THIRD_API ACC_PROV --> ACC_API THIRD_PROV --> THIRD_API AUTH_CON --> UTILS HEALTH_CON --> UTILS DOC_CON --> UTILS GATEWAY --> AUTH_API ``` **Domain Relationship Matrix:** | From Domain | To Domain | Relation Type | Strength | Description | |-------------|-----------|---------------|----------|-------------| | API Gateway | Authentication | Service Call | 9.5 | Token validation for every protected request | | API Gateway | Account Management | Service Call | 7.0 | Permission fetching for user context | | Authentication | Account Management | Service Call | 9.0 | Credential verification and permission lookup | | Health Report Center | Account Management | Service Call | 8.0 | Patient/doctor info for report association | | Health Report Center | Third-Party | Service Call | 8.5 | OSS file upload for reports | | Doctor Service | Account Management | Service Call | 7.5 | Doctor/patient profile retrieval | | Message System | Account Management | Service Call | 6.5 | Recipient contact information | | Message System | Third-Party | Service Call | 8.0 | SMS and email delivery | | All Domains | Common Utilities | Tool Support | 8.0+ | Security, encryption, context management | --- ## 4. Component View ### 4.1 Core Functional Components #### 4.1.1 Authentication & Authorization Components ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Authentication Domain Components" JWT_MGMT[JWT Token Management
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• generateAccessToken
• generateRefreshToken
• validateAndParseToken
• revokeToken] TOKEN_VAL[Token Validation Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• validateToken
• revokeToken
• checkBlacklist] SSO_SVC[SSO Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 8.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• login
• register
• refreshToken
• logout] SEC_CFG[Security Configuration
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 7.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• passwordEncoder
• securityFilterChain] end SSO_SVC --> JWT_MGMT SSO_SVC --> TOKEN_VAL TOKEN_VAL --> JWT_MGMT ``` **Key Implementation Details:** | Component | Key Classes | Responsibilities | |-----------|-------------|------------------| | JWT Token Management | `JwtUtils`, `JwtConfig`, `JwtServiceImpl` | RSA-based token signing, Redis blacklist management | | Token Validation Service | `TokenValidationServiceImpl`, `TokenValidationAPI` | Dubbo RPC endpoint for gateway token validation | | SSO Service | `SSOController`, `SSOServiceImpl`, `AuthClient`, `AccountClient` | Login orchestration, token issuance | | Security Configuration | `SecurityConfig`, `RedisConfig` | BCrypt encoding, stateless session config | #### 4.1.2 Account Management Components ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Account Domain Components" ACC_SVC[Account Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• login (multi-method)
• register
• updateUserInfo
• updatePassword] IDENTITY_SVC[User Identity Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 8.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• getDoctorInfo
• getPatientInfo
• getDoctorInfoByAccountId] PERM_SVC[Permission Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 8.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• getUserPermission
• hasPermission
• expandToPermissions] DATA_MODELS[Account Data Models
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 7.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Account, Doctor, Patient
• Role, Permission
• AccountRole] end ACC_SVC --> DATA_MODELS IDENTITY_SVC --> DATA_MODELS PERM_SVC --> DATA_MODELS ``` #### 4.1.3 Health Report Center Components ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Health Report Domain Components" REPORT_CTRL[Health Report Controller
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 8.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• uploadReport
• queryReportList
• getReportDetail] REPORT_SVC[Health Report Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• uploadReport
• queryReportList
• getReportDetail
• convertToVO] REPORT_MODELS[Report Data Models
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 7.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• HealthReport
• HealthReportVO
• UploadReportRequest
• ReportType/Status] EXCEPTION_HANDLER[Exception Handler
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 6.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• handleBusinessException
• handleValidationException
• handleMaxUploadSize] end REPORT_CTRL --> REPORT_SVC REPORT_SVC --> REPORT_MODELS REPORT_CTRL --> EXCEPTION_HANDLER ``` #### 4.1.4 Doctor Service Components ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Doctor Service Domain Components" WORKBENCH_CTRL[Doctor Workbench Controller
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 8.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• generateScheduleSuggestion
• queryScheduleList
• completeSchedule
• cancelSchedule] WORKBENCH_SVC[Doctor Workbench Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Importance: 9.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• generateScheduleSuggestion
• querySchedule

Key Processes ### 5.1 Core Functional Processes #### 5.1.1 User Authentication Flow The authentication flow implements a dual-token mechanism with comprehensive security measures: ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Client["Client Application"] A["User submits credentials
(username/phone/ID card + password)"] end subgraph Gateway["API Gateway Domain"] B["TraceAndJwtAuthFilter
Receives login request"] C["Route to auth-consumer
(login endpoints whitelisted)"] end subgraph AuthDomain["Authentication & Authorization Domain"] D["SSOController
Receives login request"] E["SSOServiceImpl
Process authentication"] K["JwtUtils.generateTokenPair()
Create Access Token (30min)
Create Refresh Token (7 days)"] L["Store Refresh Token
in Redis"] end subgraph AccountDomain["Account Management Domain"] F["AccountServiceImpl.login()
Query by username/phone hash/ID hash"] G["Verify BCrypt password"] H["Query AccountRole table
Get user role codes"] I["PermissionServiceImpl
getUserPermission()"] J["PermissionExpandUtil
Expand roles to permissions
(e.g., DOCTOR:READ, DOCTOR:WRITE)"] end A --> B B --> C C --> D D --> E E --> F F --> G G -->|"Valid"| H G -->|"Invalid"| T["Return Authentication Failed"] H --> I I --> J J --> K K --> L L --> U["Return Token Pair + User Info"] ``` **Authentication Flow Details:** | Step | Component | Action | Security Measure | |------|-----------|--------|------------------| | 1 | Gateway | Route to auth-consumer | Whitelist check for login endpoints | | 2 | SSOController | Receive credentials | Input validation | | 3 | AccountService | Query user by identifier | Hash-based lookup for phone/ID card | | 4 | AccountService | Verify password | BCrypt comparison | | 5 | PermissionService | Expand permissions | Role-to-permission mapping | | 6 | JwtUtils | Generate token pair | RSA-256 signing | | 7 | Redis | Store refresh token | TTL-based expiration | #### 5.1.2 API Request Authorization Flow Every protected API request goes through a comprehensive authorization pipeline: ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Request["Incoming Request"] V["API Request with Bearer Token"] end subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"] M["Validate JWT Token
via TokenValidationAPI"] N["Inject User Context Headers
X-User-Id, X-Username
X-User-Roles, X-User-Authorities"] end subgraph AuthProvider["Auth Provider"] P["TokenValidationServiceImpl
Validate token signature
Check expiration & blacklist"] end subgraph TargetService["Target Microservice"] O["UserInfoExtractInterceptor
Extract headers to ThreadLocal"] Q["PermissionCheckAspect
@RequireRole / @RequirePermission"] R["Business Logic Execution"] S["UserInfoManager.clearUserContext()
Cleanup ThreadLocal"] end V --> M M --> P P -->|"Valid"| N P -->|"Invalid/Expired/Blacklisted"| W["Return 401 Unauthorized"] N --> O O --> Q Q -->|"Permission Granted"| R Q -->|"Permission Denied"| X["Return 403 Forbidden"] R --> S ``` #### 5.1.3 Health Report Upload Flow The health report upload process includes multi-layer validation and cloud storage integration: ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Client["Patient Client"] A["Patient initiates report upload
(Image/PDF/Text)"] end subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"] B["JWT Token Validation"] C["Inject User Context Headers"] end subgraph Consumer["Health Report Consumer (BFF)"] D["HealthReportController.uploadReport()"] E["@RequireRole PATIENT
@RequirePermission patient:write"] F["Validate file type whitelist
(PNG, JPG, PDF)"] G["Validate file size
(max 10MB)"] H["Verify patientId matches
current user from JWT"] end subgraph Provider["Health Report Provider"] I["HealthReportServiceImpl.uploadReport()"] J["Validate request parameters"] K{"Report Type?"} end subgraph AccountService["Account Management"] L["UserIdentityAPI.getPatientInfo()"] M["Retrieve attendingDoctorId"] end subgraph ThirdParty["Third-Party Integration"] N["OSSAPI.uploadFile()"] O["Upload to Alibaba Cloud OSS"] P["Return permanent file URL"] end subgraph Persistence["Data Persistence"] Q["Build HealthReport entity"] R["Set status = PENDING
(awaiting review)"] S["Associate with attending doctor"] T["Save to MySQL via MyBatis-Plus"] end A --> B B -->|"Valid"| C B -->|"Invalid"| Z1["401 Unauthorized"] C --> D D --> E E -->|"Authorized"| F E -->|"Denied"| Z2["403 Forbidden"] F -->|"Valid type"| G F -->|"Invalid type"| Z3["400 Invalid File Type"] G -->|"Within limit"| H G -->|"Exceeds limit"| Z4["413 File Too Large"] H -->|"Ownership verified"| I H -->|"Mismatch"| Z5["403 Access Denied"] I --> J J --> L L --> M M --> K K -->|"IMAGE/PDF"| N N --> O O --> P P --> Q K -->|"TEXT"| Q Q --> R R --> S S --> T T --> U["Return Success
with Report ID"] ``` #### 5.1.4 Doctor Schedule Management Flow The doctor schedule workflow supports AI recommendations and complete lifecycle management: ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph DoctorClient["Doctor Workbench Client"] A["Doctor requests
schedule recommendations"] B["Doctor queries
existing schedules"] C["Doctor updates
schedule status"] end subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"] D["JWT Validation"] E["Inject Doctor Context"] end subgraph Consumer["Doctor Service Consumer"] F["DoctorWorkbenchController"] G["@RequireRole DOCTOR"] H["@RequirePermission doctor:read/write"] I["Override doctorId with
JWT userId (security)"] end subgraph Provider["Doctor Service Provider"] J["DoctorWorkbenchServiceImpl"] K["generateScheduleSuggestion()
(AI Mock Implementation)"] L["querySchedule()
Multi-criteria filter"] M["getScheduleDetail()

(@Version)"] ZD["Update DoctorSchedule"] ZE["Return ScheduleVO"] end A --> D B --> D C --> D D --> E E --> F F --> G G --> H H --> I I --> J J -->|"AI Suggestion"| K K --> ZE J -->|"Query"| L L --> ZE J -->|"Update Status"| N N --> Q Q --> R R -->|"To IN_PROGRESS"| T Q --> S S -->|"To COMPLETED"| T T --> ZC J -->|"Complete"| O O --> U U -->|"Yes"| V U -->|"No"| Z1["400 Diagnosis Required"] V --> W W --> X X --> ZC ZC -->|"Version Match"| ZD ZC -->|"Conflict"| Z3["409 Concurrent Update"] ZD --> ZE ``` ### 5.2 Technical Processing Workflows #### 5.2.1 Token Refresh Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Client["Client Application"] A["Access Token expired
(after 30 minutes)"] B["Submit Refresh Token
POST /auth/refresh"] end subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"] C["Route to auth-consumer
(refresh endpoint whitelisted)"] end subgraph AuthProvider["Auth Provider"] F["JwtUtils.validateRefreshToken()"] G["Decode JWT & verify signature"] H{"Token type = 'refresh'?"} I["Extract tokenId from claims"] J{"Exists in Redis?"} K["Extract userId, roles
from token claims"] end subgraph Redis["Redis Cache"] L["refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}"] M["Check token existence"] end subgraph AccountDomain["Account Management"] N["PermissionServiceImpl
getUserPermission()"] O["Fetch latest permissions
(may have changed)"] P["PermissionExpandUtil
Expand to permission strings"] end subgraph TokenGeneration["New Token Generation"] Q["JwtUtils.generateAccessToken()
New 30-min access token"] R{"Rotate refresh token?"} S["Generate new refresh token"] T["Revoke old refresh token"] U["Store new token in Redis"] end A --> B B --> C C --> F F --> G G --> H H -->|"Yes"| I H -->|"No"| X["400 Invalid Token Type"] I --> J J -->|"Check"| L L --> M M -->|"Exists"| K M -->|"Not found/Revoked"| Y["401 Token Revoked"] K --> N N --> O O --> P P --> Q Q --> R R -->|"Yes (optional)"| S S --> T T --> U U --> V["Return new token pair"] R -->|"No"| W["Return new access token only"] ``` #### 5.2.2 User Logout and Token Revocation Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Client["Client Application"] A["User initiates logout"] B["Submit access token
POST /auth/logout"] end subgraph AuthProvider["Auth Provider"] F["TokenValidationServiceImpl
revokeToken()"] G["Parse access token"] H["Extract tokenId"] I["Extract expiration time"] J["Extract userId from claims"] end subgraph AccessTokenRevocation["Access Token Blacklist"] K["JwtUtils.addToBlacklist()"] L["Calculate remaining TTL
(expiration - now)"] M["Add to Redis blacklist
blacklist:{tokenId}"] N["Set TTL = remaining validity"] end subgraph RefreshTokenRevocation["Refresh Token Cleanup"] O["JwtUtils.revokeRefreshToken()"] P["Delete from Redis
refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}"] Q{"Revoke all tokens?
(forced logout)"} R["Delete all user refresh tokens
refresh_token:{userId}:*"] end subgraph Verification["Future Request Handling"] S["Subsequent API request
with revoked token"] T["TraceAndJwtAuthFilter"] U["TokenValidationServiceImpl
validateToken()"] V{"Check blacklist"} W["Token found in blacklist"] end A --> B B --> F F --> G G --> H H --> I I --> J J --> K K --> L L --> M M --> N N --> O O --> P P --> Q Q -->|"Yes"| R R --> X["Return logout success"] Q -->|"No"| X S --> T T --> U U --> V V --> W W --> Y["Return 401 Unauthorized"] ``` ### 5.3 Data Flow Paths #### 5.3.1 User Context Propagation ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Gateway participant Service participant Interceptor participant ThreadLocal participant Controller participant AOP Gateway->>Service: HTTP Request with headers Note over Gateway,Service: X-User-Id: 123
X-Username: john
X-User-Roles: DOCTOR
X-User-Authorities: DOCTOR:READ,DOCTOR:WRITE Service->>Interceptor: preHandle() Interceptor->>Interceptor: Extract headers Interceptor->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.setUserContext() Interceptor->>Controller: Continue request Controller->>AOP: Method with @RequireRole AOP->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.getRoles() AOP->>AOP: Validate permissions Controller->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.getUserId() Note over Controller: Business logic uses context Controller-->>Interceptor: Response Interceptor->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.clearUserContext() Note over Interceptor: Prevent memory leak ``` #### 5.3.2 Sensitive Data Encryption Flow ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Write Path" PLAIN[Plain Text
phone: 13812345678] ENCRYPT[AES-CBC Encrypt] BASE64[Base64 Encode] DB_WRITE[(MySQL
encrypted value)] end subgraph "Read Path" DB_READ[(MySQL
encrypted value)] DECODE[Base64 Decode] DECRYPT[AES-CBC Decrypt] RESULT[Plain Text
phone: 13812345678] end PLAIN --> ENCRYPT --> BASE64 --> DB_WRITE DB_READ --> DECODE --> DECRYPT --> RESULT subgraph "MyBatis TypeHandler" HANDLER[SensitiveDataEncryptHandler
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Algorithm: AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding
Key: 16 bytes
IV: 16 bytes] end ``` ### 5.4 Exception Handling Mechanisms The platform implements a comprehensive exception handling strategy: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Exception Hierarchy" BASE[BaseException] BUSINESS[BusinessException
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

with error codes] RR[RRException
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Runtime business errors] AUTH[AuthenticationException
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
401 Unauthorized] PERM[PermissionException
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
403 Forbidden] end subgraph "Error Codes" TOB[ToBCodeEnum
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
@ControllerAdvice
Unified error response] end BASE --> BUSINESS BASE --> RR BASE --> AUTH BASE --> PERM BUSINESS --> TOB BUSINESS --> TOC GEH --> BUSINESS GEH --> RR GEH --> AUTH GEH --> PERM ``` **Exception Response Format:** ```json { "code": 40001, "message": "Authentication failed: Invalid credentials", "data": null, "timestamp": 1736380800000, "traceId": "abc123def456" } ``` --- ## 6. Technical Implementation ### 6.1 Core Module Implementation #### 6.1.1 JWT Token Management Implementation The JWT implementation uses RSA-256 asymmetric encryption for enhanced security: ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Access Token (30 min)" AT_HEADER[Header
alg: RS256] AT_PAYLOAD[Payload
━━━━━━━━━━━━
sub: userId
type: access
username: string
roles: array
authorities: array
iat: timestamp
exp: timestamp] AT_SIG[Signature
RSA-SHA256] end subgraph "Refresh Token (7 days)" RT_HEADER[Header
alg: RS256] RT_PAYLOAD[Payload
━━━━━━━━━━━━
sub: userId
type: refresh
jti: tokenId
roles: array
iat: timestamp
exp: timestamp] RT_SIG[Signature
RSA-SHA256] end subgraph "Redis Storage" REFRESH_STORE["auth:refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}"] BLACKLIST["auth:blacklist:{tokenId}"] PERMISSIONS["auth:permissions:{userId}"] end ``` **Key Implementation Classes:** | Class | Location | Responsibility | |-------|----------|----------------| | `JwtUtils` | `auth-provider/utils/` | Token generation, validation, blacklist management | | `JwtConfig` | `auth-provider/config/` | RSA key configuration, token expiration settings | | `TokenValidationServiceImpl` | `auth-provider/service/` | Dubbo RPC endpoint for gateway validation | #### 6.1.2 RBAC Permission Model Implementation ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Role Hierarchy" DOCTOR[DOCTOR
Code: 1] PATIENT[PATIENT
Code: 2] FAMILY[FAMILY
Code: 3] end subgraph "Actions" READ[READ
Code: 1] WRITE[WRITE
Code: 2] ALL[ALL
Code: 3] end subgraph "Permission Strings" P1["DOCTOR:READ"] P2["DOCTOR:WRITE"] P3["DOCTOR:ALL"] P4["PATIENT:READ"] P5["PATIENT:WRITE"] end DOCTOR --> P1 DOCTOR --> P2 DOCTOR --> P3 PATIENT --> P4 PATIENT --> P5 subgraph "Permission Expansion" ALL_EXP["ALL expands to:
READ + WRITE + ALL"] end ``` **Permission Expansion Logic:** ```java // PermissionExpandUtil.java public static List expandToPermissions(String roleCode, String actionCode) { List permissions = new ArrayList<>(); if ("ALL".equals(actionCode)) { permissions.add(roleCode + ":READ"); permissions.add(roleCode + ":WRITE"); permissions.add(roleCode + ":ALL"); } else { permissions.add(roleCode + ":" + actionCode); } return permissions; } ``` #### 6.1.3 Security Annotation Implementation The platform uses AOP-based declarative security: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Annotation Definition" REQ_ROLE["@RequireRole
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
value: String[]
logical: AND/OR"] REQ_PERM["@RequirePermission
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
value: String[]
logical: AND/OR"] end subgraph "AOP Aspect" ASPECT["PermissionCheckAspect
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
@Around advice
Intercepts annotated methods"] end subgraph "Context Provider" USER_MGR["UserInfoManager
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ThreadLocal storage
getRoles()
getAuthorities()"] end REQ_ROLE --> ASPECT REQ_PERM --> ASPECT ASPECT --> USER_MGR ``` **Usage Example:** ```java @RestController @RequestMapping("/doctor/workbench") public class DoctorWorkbenchController { @GetMapping("/schedule") @RequireRole("DOCTOR") @RequirePermission("doctor:read") public Result> querySchedule(QueryScheduleRequest request) { // Method only accessible to users with DOCTOR role and doctor:read permission return Result.success(workbenchService.querySchedule(request)); } } ``` ### 6.2 Key Algorithm Design #### 6.2.1 Sensitive Data Encryption Algorithm The platform uses AES-CBC encryption for sensitive fields: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Encryption Process" INPUT[Plain Text Input] KEY[AES Key
16 bytes] IV[Initialization Vector
16 bytes] AES[AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding] B64[Base64 Encoding] OUTPUT[Encrypted Output] end INPUT --> AES KEY --> AES IV --> AES AES --> B64 B64 --> OUTPUT ``` **Implementation Details:** | Parameter | Value | Purpose | |-----------|-------|---------| | Algorithm | AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding | Block cipher with padding | | Key Size | 128 bits (16 bytes) | Encryption key | | IV Size | 128 bits (16 bytes) | Initialization vector | | Encoding | Base64 | Storage-safe encoding | #### 6.2.2 Hash-Based Lookup Algorithm For encrypted fields that need to be searchable (phone, ID card): ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Storage Strategy" PLAIN[Plain Value
13812345678] ENCRYPT_PATH[Encryption Path] HASH_PATH[Hash Path] ENCRYPTED[Encrypted Value
AES-CBC + Base64] HASHED[Hash Value
SHA-256] DB[(Database)] end PLAIN --> ENCRYPT_PATH PLAIN --> HASH_PATH ENCRYPT_PATH --> ENCRYPTED HASH_PATH --> HASHED ENCRYPTED -->|"phone column"| DB HASHED -->|"phone_hash column"| DB ``` **Lookup Process:** 1. Hash the search value using SHA-256 2. Query database using the hash column (indexed) 3. Decrypt the encrypted column for display ### 6.3 Data Structure Design #### 6.3.1 User Context Data Structure ```java public class UserInfoContext { private Long userId; private String username; private List roles; private List authorities; private String traceId; private Long timestamp; } ``` #### 6.3.2 Token Claims Structure ```java public class TokenClaims { // Access Token Claims private Long sub; // User ID private String type; // "access" or "refresh" private String username; private List roles; private List authorities; private Long iat; // Issued at private Long exp; // Expiration // Refresh Token Additional Claims private String jti; // Token ID for revocation } ``` ### 6.4 Performance Optimization Strategies #### 6.4.1 Caching Strategy ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Cache Layers" L1[Local Cache
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ThreadLocal user context
Request-scoped] L2[Redis Cache
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
JWT tokens
User permissions
Session data] L3[Database
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Persistent storage
Source of truth] end L1 -->|"Miss"| L2 L2 -->|"Miss"| L3 L3 -->|"Populate"| L2 L2 -->|"Populate"| L1 ``` **Cache TTL Configuration:** | Cache Type | TTL | Purpose | |------------|-----|---------| | Access Token | 30 minutes | Short-lived API authorization | | Refresh Token | 7 days | Session extension | | User Permissions | 5 minutes | Permission caching | | Blacklist Entry | Token remaining TTL | Token invalidation | #### 6.4.2 Connection Pool Optimization ```yaml # Database Connection Pool spring: datasource: hikari: maximum-pool-size: 20 minimum-idle: 5 connection-timeout: 30000 idle-timeout: 600000 max-lifetime: 1800000 # Redis Connection Pool spring: redis: lettuce: pool: max-active: 16 max-idle: 8 min-idle: 2 max-wait: 5000ms ``` #### 6.4.3 Rate Limiting Strategy ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Sentinel Flow Control" RULE1[Global Rate Limit
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1000 QPS per service] RULE2[API-Level Limits
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
/auth/login: 100 QPS
/health/upload: 50 QPS] RULE3[User-Level Limits
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SMS: 5/minute/user
Email: 10/minute/user] end subgraph "Circuit Breaker" CB[Degrade Rules
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Error ratio > 50%
Slow ratio > 30%
Recovery: 10 seconds] end ``` --- ## 7. Deployment Architecture ### 7.1 Runtime Environment Requirements | Component | Requirement | Recommended | |-----------|-------------|-------------| | **JDK** | Java 17+ | OpenJDK 17 LTS | | **Memory** | 2GB minimum per service | 4GB per service | | **CPU** | 2 cores minimum | 4 cores per service | | **Disk** | 20GB per service | SSD recommended | | **Network** | 1Gbps | Low latency between services | ### 7.2 Deployment Topology Structure ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Load Balancer" LB[Nginx / Cloud LB] end subgraph "Gateway Cluster" GW1[Gateway Instance 1] GW2[Gateway Instance 2] end subgraph "Service Cluster" subgraph "Auth Services" AUTH1[Auth Provider 1] AUTH2[Auth Provider 2] end subgraph "Account Services" ACC1[Account Provider 1] ACC2[Account Provider 2] end subgraph "Business Services" HEALTH1[Health Provider 1] DOC1[Doctor Provider 1] MSG1[Message Provider 1] end end subgraph "Infrastructure" NACOS_CLUSTER[Nacos Cluster
3 nodes] REDIS_CLUSTER[Redis Cluster
3 master + 3 slave] MYSQL_CLUSTER[MySQL
Master-Slave] end LB --> GW1 LB --> GW2 GW1 --> NACOS_CLUSTER GW2 --> NACOS_CLUSTER AUTH1 --> NACOS_CLUSTER AUTH2 --> NACOS_CLUSTER ACC1 --> NACOS_CLUSTER ACC2 --> NACOS_CLUSTER AUTH1 --> REDIS_CLUSTER AUTH2 --> REDIS_CLUSTER ACC1 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER ACC2 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER HEALTH1 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER DOC1 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER MSG1 --> MYSQL_CLUSTER ``` ### 7.3 Scalability Design #### 7.3.1 Horizontal Scaling Strategy ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Scaling Triggers" CPU[CPU > 70%] MEM[Memory > 80%] QPS[QPS > threshold] LATENCY[P99 > 500ms] end subgraph "Scaling Actions" SCALE_OUT[Scale Out
Add instances] SCALE_IN[Scale In
Remove instances] end subgraph "Service Discovery" NACOS[Nacos
Auto-registration] end CPU --> SCALE_OUT MEM --> SCALE_OUT QPS --> SCALE_OUT LATENCY --> SCALE_OUT SCALE_OUT --> NACOS SCALE_IN --> NACOS ``` #### 7.3.2 Service Scaling Recommendations | Service | Min Instances | Max Instances | Scaling Metric | |---------|---------------|---------------|----------------| | API Gateway | 2 | 10 | QPS, CPU | | Auth Provider | 2 | 8 | QPS, CPU | | Account Provider | 2 | 6 | QPS, CPU | | Health Provider | 2 | 8 | QPS, Memory | | Doctor Provider | 2 | 6 | QPS, CPU | | Message Provider | 2 | 6 | Queue depth | ### 7.4 Monitoring and Operations #### 7.4.1 Monitoring Architecture ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Application Metrics" ACTUATOR[Spring Boot Actuator

Revocation** | Redis blacklist with TTL | Strong | --- ## 9. Conclusion The **ChronicCareAIPlatform** demonstrates a well-architected microservices system with: 1. **Clear Separation of Concerns**: The three-tier module pattern (API/Consumer/Provider) ensures clean boundaries between HTTP handling, business logic, and data access. 2. **Robust Security**: Multi-layered security with JWT authentication, RBAC authorization, AOP-based permission enforcement, and data encryption at rest. 3. **Scalable Design**: Dubbo RPC with Nacos service discovery enables horizontal scaling of individual services. 4. **Maintainable Codebase**: Shared utilities module provides consistent patterns for user context, security annotations, and exception handling. 5. **Production-Ready Infrastructure**: Integration with Sentinel for rate limiting, Redis for caching/sessions, and cloud services for storage and messaging. The architecture aligns well with the documented design patterns and provides a solid foundation for a healthcare management platform serving patients, doctors, and administrators. Future enhancements should focus on implementing actual AI capabilities, adding comprehensive distributed tracing, and enhancing API documentation for developer experience. --- **Document Metadata:** - **Generated**: 2025-01-09 - **Confidence Score**: 8.5/10 - **Architecture Maturity**: Production-Ready - **Recommended Review Cycle**: Quarterly
